title: Methods for Handling Deadlocks
description: A flowchart illustrating the primary strategies an operating system can employ to manage deadlocks, from prevention to recovery.
relationships: Prevention is a proactive method that constrains resource requests
Avoidance is a proactive method that requires knowledge of future processes
Detection & Recovery is a reactive method that allows deadlocks to occur then acts
Ignoring is a policy choice for environments where deadlocks are rare or inconsequential
